name: AllGoRhythms

notes: Reconciled all 82 frozen rows against 73 restaurant-issued main-menu rows and 16 event-menu presentations, plus 82 linked SpotApps rows. The two current surfaces collapse to 76 distinct food formulations and 171 retained source presentations.

Eight frozen rows are homepage, price, promotional, or alcohol-heading artifacts; Signature Kabob Sizzle/Sizzler is one formulation across service menus; Quesadilla, Fries, and Gulab Groove were absent from the frozen app catalog.

No complete recipe-level allergen matrix or item-level cross-contact disclosure was found. Positive claims are limited to fixed ingredients and unavoidable named formats in the restaurant-issued menu text; all unsupported rows remain unavailable.
The general statement that the menu marks gluten-free/vegan options and asks guests to tell a server about allergies is accommodation context, not an item-level negative or cross-contact claim.
Selectable paneer, shrimp, fish, cheese dip, butter, proteins, sauces, and sides are not promoted into fixed allergens for the parent formulation.

The configured generic-website path fetches the restaurant homepage, the restaurant-issued food-menu HTML, and the restaurant-linked SpotApps Nuxt catalog. extractSpotAppsNuxtMenuItems correctly emits 82 linked vendor rows, but generic homepage/DOM extraction and later source merging admitted non-product cards and kept the Sizzle/Sizzler service-menu spelling as separate formulations.

The frozen ingredient pass promoted lexical hits from optional choices and ambiguous descriptive language into fixed allergens. The audited catalog replaces that output with per-formulation fixed-source adjudication.
The quality classifier also treated Gulab Groove as alcohol bleed because rose water matched the rosé vocabulary; the dessert-category food guard now preserves that published dessert.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 404 for both /631994 and /1139171; they are embedded menu identifiers/relative navigation targets, while both menus remain present in the captured /food-menu document.
notes: The direct 404 routes are not used as current menu evidence.

findings.summary: The frozen 82-row app catalog contains eight non-product artifacts, one duplicated formulation, and omits three current food formulations.
findings.resolution: Replaced the entry with 76 current formulations, retained all 171 source presentations, excluded the eight artifacts, consolidated the duplicate, and restored Quesadilla, Fries, and Gulab Groove.

findings.summary: Twenty-six frozen formulations have incorrect fixed allergen sets: 16 underreports and 10 overreports.
findings.resolution: Applied item-level fixed-ingredient adjudication, added omitted signals such as pizza/pasta/bread/dessert formats, and removed optional or unsupported paneer, shrimp, dairy, and breading signals.

findings.summary: Gulab Groove was quarantined as alcohol-only menu bleed because its rose-water dessert description matched the rosé token.
findings.resolution: Added a narrowly scoped dessert-category food guard to the shared alcohol-bleed classifier and covered the published item in a focused test.

repairs.summary: Added a source-backed AllGoRhythms catalog/reconciliation fixture, a guarded generated repair, and the narrow shared classifier correction needed to retain Gulab Groove.

notes: AllGoRhythms identity confirmed at 21035 Dulles Town Circle, Sterling, Virginia through its restaurant-issued site and linked ordering catalog.
A scope comparison of the actual generated repository before/after persistence showed only osm-allgorhythms-12234974276 changed.
